notable_allows: awards-and-festivals for major festivals (Venice, Cannes, Oscars, etc.)

json_ld.notes: No JSON-LD found on film pages via curl. Content rendered via Next.js SSR with __NEXT_DATA__ but no explicit ld+json script tags.

ssr.evidence: __NEXT_DATA__ and meta description present for both normal and Googlebot UAs

mubi.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Sitemap-first via feeds.mubi.com/sitemap.xml. Respect 5s crawl-delay. Use film sub-sitemaps for content URLs. SSR content available without JS rendering. No JSON-LD but rich meta tags. Estimated 150k+ film pages across 15 shards.

json_ld.notes: Rich TVSeries JSON-LD with aggregateRating, genre, actors, countryOfOrigin. BreadcrumbList for navigation.

ssr.evidence: Full SSR with __NEXT_DATA__, title, meta description, and JSON-LD all rendered server-side

viki.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Excellent crawlability. Sitemap-driven with rich JSON-LD on content pages. TV sitemap has ~1871 show URLs. Full SSR via Next.js. Parse JSON-LD for structured metadata (ratings, cast, genres). No crawl-delay specified.

sitemap.notes: Both sitemap URLs declared in robots.txt but Cloudflare challenge blocks all curl/bot access

json_ld.notes: Cannot verify - Cloudflare bot protection blocks all non-browser requests including Googlebot UA

ssr.evidence: Cloudflare 'Just a moment...' challenge returned for all UAs including Googlebot

crunchyroll.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Highly restrictive. Cloudflare bot protection blocks all programmatic access. Would require headless browser with Cloudflare bypass. Consider using their public API if available, or scraping cached Google results. Most difficult platform to crawl in this set.

sitemap.notes: Entertainment gb-0 shard alone has 10000 URLs. Includes hreflang alternates for GB/IE.

json_ld.notes: JSON-LD with @graph containing TVSeries type. Present with both normal and Googlebot UAs.

ssr.evidence: Full SSR with title, JSON-LD rendered server-side. Content-rich HTML in response.

nowtv.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Excellent crawlability. Large sitemap index with well-organized content shards by region and category. Full SSR with JSON-LD (TVSeries). Start with entertainment and movies sitemaps. Respect hreflang for GB/IE variants.

sitemap.notes: Dynamic sitemap contains show/content URLs with hreflang alternates for en, en-ca, en-us

json_ld.notes: No JSON-LD found. Geo-restricted - returns 'Location not Supported' from non-UK/US IPs. Has meta description tag.

ssr.evidence: Returns title and meta description but geo-blocks content. Show pages return 'Location not Supported' or 'Page not found' from outside supported regions.

britbox.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Sitemap-driven with 13k+ content URLs in dynamic sitemap. Geo-restriction is main obstacle - need UK/US proxy for content pages. No JSON-LD. Parse hreflang variants from sitemap for multi-region content.

content_types: landing pages, login, legal, sport, promos
sitemap.notes: Very small sitemap with only top-level pages. No individual show/movie URLs in sitemap.

json_ld.notes: No JSON-LD found on homepage. Minimal SSR content.

ssr.framework: SPA (noscript tag present suggesting client-side rendering)
ssr.evidence: Title rendered but no meta description or structured content in HTML. Likely relies on client-side rendering.

stan.com.au.recommended_crawl_strategy: Poor crawlability. Tiny sitemap (26 URLs) with no content pages. SPA-style rendering. No JSON-LD. Would need headless browser to render content pages and discover show/movie URLs through navigation. Consider API discovery or Google cache scraping.

sitemap.notes: Large sitemap with 277 content shards. Bilingual (en/fr) content.

json_ld.notes: No JSON-LD found. Client-side rendered SPA with React (id='root').

ssr.evidence: Only id='root' div found in HTML response - pure client-side rendering. No title, no meta description, no structured data in initial HTML.

crave.ca.recommended_crawl_strategy: Sitemap provides massive URL inventory (~138k URLs). However, pages are pure client-side SPA (React) with no SSR. Must use headless browser for content extraction. No JSON-LD. Sitemap is the primary discovery mechanism.

notable_disallows: Default blocks all bots, then explicitly allows major search engine bots (Googlebot, Bingbot, Applebot, etc.)
robots_txt.notes: Disallow: / for User-agent: * but Allow: / for named bots. Blocks logout and payment pages.

content_types: shows-episodes (6 ar shards x 10000, 6+ en shards)
sitemap.notes: Bilingual ar/en content. Episode-level granularity. Movies sitemap has ~1538 URLs. Episode shards have up to 10000 URLs each.

json_ld.notes: JSON-LD present on movie pages. Rich meta tags including og:url, twitter:card, canonical links, and hreflang for ar/en/fr.

ssr.evidence: __NEXT_DATA__ present, meta description and JSON-LD rendered server-side for both normal and Googlebot UAs. Full content titles like 'Watch The Comeback Trial - MBC Shahid'.

shahid.mbc.net.recommended_crawl_strategy: Good crawlability for named bots. Use Googlebot UA or similar recognized bot. Sitemap provides ~100k+ URLs across movies and episode-level content. Full Next.js SSR with JSON-LD. Bilingual ar/en content. Respect robot selective access pattern.

content_types: VOD by country/language (sg-en, sg-zh, id-id, hk-zh, my-ms, ph-en, etc.)
Middle East regions (ye-ar, sa-ar, kw-ar, etc.)
sitemap.notes: 48 regional sub-sitemaps covering SE Asia, Middle East, and Africa. Singapore alone has ~6066 URLs. Multi-language per region.

json_ld.notes: No JSON-LD found. Geo-restricted - returns 'No Service' page from outside supported regions.

ssr.evidence: Returns 'Viu | No Service' from outside supported regions even with Googlebot UA. Content likely SSR within supported regions.

viu.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Large sitemap inventory across 48 regional sub-sitemaps (~100k+ URLs). Strict geo-restriction requires regional proxies (SG, MY, PH, HK, ID, etc.). No JSON-LD detected. Content URLs organized by country/language. Need per-region crawling strategy.

sitemap.notes: 53 gzipped sub-sitemaps. Last modified dates from 2021 - sitemap may be stale.

json_ld.notes: JSON-LD found on homepage (3 blocks). Navigation/organizational schema rather than content-specific.

ssr.evidence: Homepage returns full SSR with title, meta description, noscript fallback. Play pages return 400 errors. Content pages may have different rendering.

iq.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Sitemap available but potentially stale (last modified 2021). 53 gzipped sub-sitemaps need decompression. SSR with Next.js on homepage. JSON-LD is organizational only (no content-level schema). Play URLs may return 400 - use album URLs instead. Open robots.txt (Allow: /).

sitemap.notes: Massive number of sub-sitemaps (4073) but individually small. Last modified dates range from 2022 to 2025.

json_ld.notes: Multiple JSON-LD blocks on pages including VideoObject for content. Present with both normal and Googlebot UAs.

ssr.evidence: Full SSR with title, meta description, og tags, and JSON-LD all rendered server-side. Rich SEO metadata.

wetv.vip.recommended_crawl_strategy: Good crawlability. Very large sitemap index (4073 sub-sitemaps) but small per-shard. Full SSR with rich JSON-LD (VideoObject). Parse JSON-LD for structured content data. Multi-language content (en, th, id, ms). Open access with standard disallows.

robots_txt.notes: Notably disallows /shows, /series, /movies - blocks content listing pages. MauiBot fully blocked.

sitemap.notes: Mostly help/support pages per country. Main content pages (/shows, /series, /movies) are disallowed in robots.txt. NOTE: Showmax is transitioning to DStv Stream - content pages redirect to migration notice.

json_ld.notes: JSON-LD script tag exists but contains empty object {}. Platform is shutting down/migrating to DStv Stream.

ssr.evidence: Full Next.js SSR with __NEXT_DATA__, meta description, noscript. However, content pages show 'Showmax has a new home' migration notice. Built on 'ott-peacock-iceberg' platform (same as NowTV).

showmax.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: LOW PRIORITY - Platform is actively migrating to DStv Stream. Content pages return migration notices. Robots.txt blocks /shows, /series, /movies. Sitemap mostly contains help pages. JSON-LD is empty. Consider targeting DStv Stream instead.

robots_txt.notes: Explicitly blocks AI training bots (CCBot, GPTBot, Google-Extended). No general User-agent: * restrictions beyond these.

content_types: video pages organized by date, with image and video extensions
sitemap.estimated_total_urls: ~500000+ (daily sub-sitemaps dating back years, ~1000 URLs per day shard)
sitemap.notes: Enormous video-focused sitemap. Sub-sitemaps organized by date (e.g., /2026/04/02_1.xml). Includes Google video and image sitemap extensions with thumbnails, content_loc, duration, subscription requirements.

json_ld.notes: JSON-LD on homepage with WebSite schema including SearchAction. Content pages may have additional schema. Present with both normal and Googlebot UAs.

ssr.evidence: Homepage title is generic 'Logo Globoplay' suggesting SPA shell. JSON-LD is injected server-side via data-react-helmet. Content likely requires JS rendering.

globoplay.globo.com.recommended_crawl_strategy: Massive content inventory (~500k+ video URLs). Sitemap is primary discovery mechanism with rich video sitemap extensions (thumbnails, content_loc, duration). Blocks AI bots explicitly. JSON-LD available server-side. SPA rendering means headless browser may be needed for full content. Portuguese language content.

best_crawlability.reason: Full SSR, rich JSON-LD (TVSeries), clean sitemaps, no geo-blocking
Full SSR, JSON-LD (TVSeries), 50k+ URLs in organized sitemaps
Full SSR, JSON-LD, 100k+ URLs, bilingual. Requires recognized bot UA.

moderate_crawlability.reason: Good SSR, large sitemaps (~150k URLs), but no JSON-LD and 5s crawl-delay
13k+ URLs in sitemap, SSR, but geo-restricted content
Massive inventory, video sitemap extensions, but SPA rendering and blocks AI bots
SSR homepage, basic JSON-LD, but stale sitemaps and play pages return 400
Large sitemap (~138k URLs), but pure SPA with no SSR or JSON-LD

poor_crawlability.reason: Cloudflare blocks all programmatic access including Googlebot UA
Tiny sitemap (26 URLs), SPA rendering, no JSON-LD, no content URLs
Large sitemap but strict geo-restriction returns 'No Service' for all regions
Migrating to DStv Stream, content pages return migration notices, empty JSON-LD
